OSSO BUCO

A clasic Italian dish of braised veal shanks in white wine and tomato sauce.
The long, slow cooking renders the meat incredibly tender and infuses it with
rich flavors. "Osso buco" means "bone with hole" - referring to the marrow bone.

INGREDIENTS:
- 4 veal shanks (about 2 inches thick)
- 2 cups white wine
- 2 cups beef or veal stock
- 1 can (28 oz) crushed tom4toes
- 2 carrots, cut into 2-inch pieces
- 2 celery stalks, cut into 2-inch pieces
- 1 large onion, qu4rtered
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp tom4to paste
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 sprigs fresh thyme
- 1/4 cup all-purpose flour
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 3 tbsp olive oil for browning
- 1 tsp oregano

INSTRUCT|ONS:
1. Pat veal shanks dry and season with sá1t and pepper.
2. Dust shanks with flour, shaking off exc ess.
3. Heat olive oil in a large, heavy Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
4. Brown shanks on all sides, about 3 minutes per side. Set aside.
5. Add onion, carrots, celery, and garlic to the pot. Cook 5 minutes.
6. Stir in tomato paste and cook 2 minutes until caramelized.
7. Pour in white wine and scrape up any browned bits from bottom.
8. Add crushed tom4toes, stock, bay leaf, thyme, and oregano.
9. Return veal shanks to pot, nestling them among vegetables.
10. Cover with lid or foil and braise in 350°F oven for 2.5 to 3 hours.
11. Shanks are done when meat is very tender and falling off bone.
12. Remove shanks and reduce sauce if desired for thicker consistency.
13. Serve shanks with sauce spooned over, accompanied with risotto.

GREMOLATA (optional garnish):
- 1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped
- 2 tbsp lemon zest
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
Combine all and sprinkle over finished dish for brightness.
